issue_title: fix(test): wrong description in guard test files
issue_body:
## Summary

Both `isNavigatorPermissionsSupported.test.js` and `isNavigatorMediaDevicesSupported.test.js` describe the "is supported" test case as `'returns false as permissions is not supported'` while actually asserting `toBeTruthy()`.

## Root cause

`src/__tests__/isNavigatorPermissionsSupported.test.js` and `src/__tests__/isNavigatorMediaDevicesSupported.test.js` — copy-paste error in the `it()` description inside the "implemented" describe block.

## Impact

Test reporters show a passing test labelled "returns false" that is actually testing that the function returns true. Makes CI output and failure messages misleading.

## Expected behavior

The `it()` description should read `'returns true as permissions is supported'`.
